name: Vulnerabilities by Asset Criticality - by Host
natural_language_question: How many critical vulnerabilities exist on our most critical assets, and how has that changed?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the qualys_host_detections table
Filter records to those where severity is CRITICAL and status indicates the vulnerability is open (use the status column)
Join to the qualys_hosts table on qualys_hosts.id = qualys_host_detections.host_id to bring in host attributes (ip, os)
Group by qualys_host_detections.host_id and the detection date (derive a daily snapshot from last_found_datetime) to produce a daily count of critical open vulnerabilities per host
Produce a 30-day rolling series and compare the latest 30-day total to the prior 30-day total to show trend direction
implementation_note: Requires daily snapshots derived from qualys_host_detections.last_found_datetime; gold table gold_vulnerabilities_weekly_by_host recommended for reporting

name: Vulnerabilities by Asset Criticality - by OS
natural_language_question: How many critical vulnerabilities exist on our most critical assets, and how has that changed?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the qualys_host_detections table
Filter to records where severity is CRITICAL and status indicates open
Join to qualys_hosts on qualys_hosts.id = qualys_host_detections.host_id to obtain qualys_hosts.os
Group by qualys_hosts.os and the detection snapshot date (daily) to produce a daily count per OS
Aggregate daily series to weekly for the gold table and compare week-over-week to surface trends
implementation_note: OS-level grouping requires join to qualys_hosts; recommended gold table gold_vulnerabilities_weekly_by_os

name: Vulnerability Fix Backlog by Host
natural_language_question: Which teams have the largest backlog of unresolved vulnerabilities and how has each team's backlog trended over the last 90 days?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the qualys_host_detections table
Filter to records where status indicates the vulnerability is still open
For each host_id, compute days since first observed using last_found_datetime and produce a daily snapshot row per open finding
Group by host_id and snapshot date to produce daily open vulnerability counts per host and compute average days open per host over the period
Roll up daily snapshots into weekly aggregates for backlog reporting in the gold table
implementation_note: Team-level assignment requires additional host-to-team mapping outside current tables; current recommendation produces host-level backlog as a proxy

name: Vulnerability Fix Backlog Trend (90d)
natural_language_question: Which teams have the largest backlog of unresolved vulnerabilities and how has each team's backlog trended over the last 90 days?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the qualys_host_detections table
Filter to open findings using the status column
Generate a daily snapshot per finding using last_found_datetime as the observation date
For each snapshot date compute total open findings and average days open across all hosts for that date
Produce a 90-day series and surface trend lines (current 90-day vs prior 90-day) in the gold 90-day trend table
implementation_note: Produces time-series suitable for trend dashboards; team attribution requires external mapping

name: CVE Exploitability Metrics - Snyk high CVSS
natural_language_question: How many of our open vulnerabilities are known to be exploited in the wild, and what is their EPSS distribution?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the snyk_issues table
Filter to records where v1.issue.cvssScore is present and at or above a high threshold (e.g., CVSS >= 7.0) to identify higher-risk findings
Group by the CVSS band and the issue discovery date (derive a daily snapshot if discovery timestamp is available) to produce daily counts
Aggregate daily series into weekly counts for the gold table and produce EPSS-proxy distributions using CVSS bands
implementation_note: Snyk does not provide EPSS in the available schema; CVSS bands are used as a proxy for exploitability. Gold table suggested: gold_snyk_vulnerabilities_daily

natural_language_question: How many of our open vulnerabilities are known to be exploited in the wild, and what is their EPSS distribution?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the snyk_issues table
Filter to records with a present v1.issue.cvssScore to identify scored issues
Group by v1.issue.language and issue discovery date to produce daily counts per language
Aggregate daily counts into weekly series to show trends per language in the gold table
implementation_note: Language-level trends can help prioritize remediation in language-specific teams

name: Asset Software Licensing Compliance
natural_language_question: What percentage of installed software is covered by valid licenses by vendor and site, and where are the largest concentrations of unlicensed software?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the qualys_applications table
Normalize application identity using name and version to derive unique installed software records per host (join to qualys_hosts for host context via host_id)
Group by application name/version and host snapshot date to produce daily unique installed counts per application
Aggregate daily series into weekly counts and surface top applications by installation count in the gold application inventory table
implementation_note: License-specific columns are not present in qualys_applications; this metric produces an installation inventory that supports later licensing reconciliation

name: Asset Agent Coverage
natural_language_question: What percentage of our assets have an active agent, and which sites have the most coverage gaps?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the qualys_hosts table
Define host inventory snapshots by first_found_date and derive a daily host list
From qualys_host_detections, determine which hosts have at least one recent detection in the last 30 days and join that indicator to qualys_hosts on id = host_id
Compute the daily percent of hosts with at least one detection (coverage%) and aggregate into weekly series for reporting
implementation_note: This produces a coverage proxy using presence of detections; true agent tables would improve accuracy

name: Asset Open Ports Summary - by Application (proxy)
natural_language_question: Which assets have the most open ports, and are there any newly observed ports in the last 7 days?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the qualys_applications table
Use qualys_applications.name and host_id to identify applications present on each host as a proxy for exposure
Join to qualys_hosts on qualys_hosts.id = qualys_applications.host_id to bring in host context (ip, os)
Group by host_id and application name with a daily snapshot to produce counts of applications per host and identify recently observed application installs in the last 7 days
Aggregate daily snapshots to weekly summaries in the gold table to support trending of exposed assets by application
kpis_covered: Application count per host (proxy for exposure)
New application installs in last 7 days
implementation_note: Port-level data is not available in the resolved schemas; this recommendation uses application presence as a proxy for exposure. If port scan data is added, replace the source table with the port dataset
